name: Emergency Situation
responses: This sounds urgent. Don't wait for authorization. Seek care immediately. Your health comes first.
Go to the emergency room or urgent care now. I'm noting this as an emergency. Go.
Stop waiting. Get help now. I'll handle the paperwork. Your safety matters most.

name: Child Emergency
responses: Your child comes first. Go to the emergency room now. Don't wait for authorization. Go.
This is urgent. Take your child to the ER immediately. I'm overriding the authorization.
Stop waiting. Get your child care now. I'll handle everything else.

name: Heart Attack Symptoms
responses: These are signs of a heart attack. Call emergency services immediately. Do not wait. Chew an aspirin if you have one.
Chest pain with these symptoms is a medical emergency. Call 911/emergency services now. Do not drive yourself.
This could be a heart attack. Get emergency help now. Sit down, stay calm, and wait for help to arrive.

name: Stroke Symptoms
responses: These are signs of a stroke. Call emergency services immediately. Do not wait. Every minute counts.
This sounds like a stroke. Call 911/emergency services right now. Do not drive yourself. Get help immediately.
Stroke is a medical emergency. Call emergency services now. Note the time symptoms started. Get help immediately.

name: Severe Allergic Reaction
responses: This sounds like a severe allergic reaction. Use an EpiPen if available. Call emergency services immediately.
Difficulty breathing from an allergic reaction is an emergency. Call 911/emergency services now. Use epinephrine if you have it.
This is anaphylaxis. Get emergency help now. Lie down and elevate legs if you feel faint. Do not wait.

name: Severe Bleeding
responses: This is severe bleeding. Apply direct pressure with a clean cloth. Call emergency services immediately.
Severe bleeding is a medical emergency. Apply pressure, use a tourniquet if you have one. Call for help now.
Keep pressure on the wound. Call emergency services. Do not remove any object embedded in the wound.

name: Seizure
responses: If someone is having a seizure, protect them from injury. Clear the area. Do NOT put anything in their mouth. Time the seizure. Call emergency services if it lasts more than 5 minutes or if they have trouble breathing.
For a seizure: stay calm, protect their head, move objects away. Call emergency services if it's the first seizure, lasts over 5 minutes, or if they're injured.
Seizure: place them on their side, cushion their head. Do not restrain. Call emergency services immediately.

name: Choking
responses: Choking is an emergency. Perform abdominal thrusts (Heimlich maneuver). Call emergency services immediately.
If someone is choking: stand behind them, make a fist above their navel, pull inward and upward. Call for help.
If you're alone and choking: call emergency services, then perform abdominal thrusts on yourself using a chair or counter edge.

name: Severe Burns
responses: For severe burns: cool the burn with cool water, cover with clean cloth. Do not apply ice. Call emergency services.
Severe burns require emergency care. Keep the person warm, cover the burn loosely. Do not break blisters. Get help now.
Cool the burn, remove tight items, cover with a sterile dressing. Call emergency services immediately.

name: Difficulty Breathing
responses: Difficulty breathing is an emergency. Sit upright, use an inhaler if you have one. Call emergency services immediately.
If you're having trouble breathing, get emergency help now. Sit upright and try to stay calm. Call for help.
Breathing problems need immediate attention. Call emergency services. Use your rescue inhaler if you have one.

name: Unconscious Person
responses: If someone is unconscious, check if they're breathing. If not, start CPR. Call emergency services immediately.
Unconscious person: check for breathing, place in recovery position if breathing, call emergency services.
For an unconscious person: shout for help, check breathing, call emergency services. Do not move them if injured.

name: Vitality Points Query
keywords: how do i earn vitality points
responses: What specific activity are you interested in? You can earn points through exercise, healthy eating, and health checks.
Vitality points come from staying active, eating well, and regular health checks. What would you like to focus on?
You can earn points through gym workouts, healthy food purchases, and completing health assessments. What's your favorite way to stay healthy?

name: Vitality Status Concern
responses: Status changes can feel like a loss. What matters most to you about your status?
You've been consistent. Your health journey matters more than any status. What's one thing you're proud of?
I hear that. When you've worked hard for something, losing it is hard. Let's look at what you need to maintain it.
Your loyalty matters. What would make this feel more fair to you?

name: Vitality Program Changes
responses: Program changes can be disorienting. What part is most confusing?
Change is hard, especially when it affects your routine. What was working well for you before?
Let's look at how the new rules apply to what you're already doing. What activity do you normally do for points?

name: Vitality Loyalty Concern
responses: Your loyalty matters. When rules change, it can feel like a breach of trust. What would recognition look like to you?
Years of consistency is real. That deserves to be acknowledged. What matters most about your status to you?
You've been with Discovery for years. That's a relationship, not just a membership. What's changed that feels unfair?

name: Premium Increase Concern
keywords: why did my premium go up
responses: Premium increases are stressful. Let's look at what changed and what options might be available.
When costs go up unexpectedly, it's hard. What's your biggest concern about the increase?
Let's understand why your premium increased. Would you like me to help you look at plan options?

name: Billing Debt Crisis
responses: Financial pressure is real. Discovery has payment plan arrangements that can help. Let's explore what support is available.
You're not alone in this. Many members need help with payments. There are options.
This is stressful. Let's look at what payment arrangements are possible.

name: Claim Denial
responses: A denial feels personal. Let's look at the reason together. This is about the policy, not about you.
Being denied is hard. Let's understand why and what your options are for appeal.
You did everything right. Let's look at the reason for denial and the next steps.

name: Claim Delay
responses: Waiting for a claim is frustrating. Let's check what might be causing the delay.

Claims can get stuck. Let's look into what might be holding it up.

responses: This is an emergency. Go to the ER now. Don't wait for authorization. Your health comes first.
Stop waiting. Get care now. I'm noting this as an emergency. Go.
Your health matters more than paperwork. Go to the emergency room. I'll handle the authorization.

name: Threshold Benefit
responses: The threshold benefit can be confusing. Let's explain how it works for your plan.
Think of the threshold as a safety net. Once you reach it, cover changes. Let me explain.
The threshold is where your cover switches from savings to hospital cover. Let's look at where you are.

name: Hospital Authorization
responses: Hospital admissions need pre-authorization. Let's check what's needed for your procedure.
Before you go to hospital, we need approval. Let's start that process.
Let's make sure you have everything ready for your hospital admission.
